subject: Sociology
title: State Crime
description: Learn State Crime in one minute.

hook.text: Governments torture, spy on citizens, and start illegal wars. Who arrests them? Usually no one.

buildup.text: State crime refers to illegal or harmful actions committed by governments or their agents.

discovery.text: Examples span genocide, mass surveillance, corruption, and police brutality — all done under official authority.

twist.text: States write the laws and control enforcement. The biggest criminal has the power to define what crime is.

climax.text: State crime is the hardest to prosecute because the criminal and the prosecutor are the same institution.

punchline.text: The law-maker can be the law-breaker.

quiz.question: Why is state crime difficult to prosecute?
options: The state controls both law-making and enforcement
